source: CIS AZURE KUBERNETES SERVICE (AKS) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF_updated_20250825_190647.json

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host IPC namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostIPC flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's IPC namespace can use IPC to interact with processes outside the container. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host IPC namespace. If you need to run containers which require hostIPC, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostIPC: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of hostIPC containers Search for the hostIPC Flag: In the YAML output, look for the hostIPC setting under the spec section to check if it is set to true. kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostIPC == true) | "\(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name)"' OR kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostIPC == true) | select(.metadata.namespace != "kube- system" and .metadata.namespace != "gatekeeper-system" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-arc" and .metadata.namespace != "azure- extensions-usage-system") | "\(.metadata.name) \(.metadata.namespace)"' When creating a Pod Security Policy, ["kube-system", "gatekeeper-system", "azure-arc", "azure-extensions-usage-system"] namespaces are excluded by default. This command retrieves all pods across all namespaces in JSON format, then uses jq to filter out those with the hostIPC flag set to true, and finally formats the output to show the namespace and name of each matching pod.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostIPC containers. Pod Security Policies and Assignments can be found by searching for Policies in the Azure Portal. A detailed step-by-step guide can be found here: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/policy-for- kubernetes Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostIPC containers. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/policy-for- kubernetes 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-admission/ 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-psa
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostIPC: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.source: CIS AZURE KUBERNETES SERVICE (AKS) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF_updated_20250825_190647.json

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions specific API and CLI commands which can be directly utilized to check for compliance. 
- The approach to use `kubectl` commands, in particular, provides a straightforward way to check for the presence of the `hostIPC` flag in the specification of pods across namespaces.
- The requirement to check pod security policies and configurations applicable to Kubernetes clusters aligns with automated capacity using existing Azure CLI and Kubernetes tooling.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_hostipc_restriction_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would use the Azure CLI and Kubernetes' `kubectl` to gather information about pods and their configurations in the AKS cluster.
- Specifically, it would enumerate all pods across all namespaces and check for the `hostIPC` setting in their specifications. 
- The relevant command is: 
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostIPC == true) | select(.metadata.namespace != "kube-system" and .metadata.namespace != "gatekeeper-system" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-arc" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-extensions-usage-system") | "\(.metadata.name) \(.metadata.namespace)"'
  ```
- Pass/Fail criteria would be determined based on the absence of pods where `spec.hostIPC: true` in non-excluded namespaces.
- If the output from the above command contains any entries, it indicates a failure of policy compliance which allows `hostIPC` pods; if the output is empty, it confirms compliance.
- Example API endpoint would utilize the Kubernetes API to retrieve pod specifications, ensuring health and permissions regarding the `hostIPC` usage are properly configured and respected.
